core_model.structure_summary: A first-order-in-time, second-order-in-space linear diffusion equation solved piecewise on adjoining domains, with the pieces coupled by continuity of the field and of its (coefficient-weighted) first spatial derivative across the interfaces.
core_model.canonical_form: u^{(j)}_t = \kappa_j\, u^{(j)}_{xx}, \quad x\in(a_j,b_j); \qquad u^{(j)}(x,0)=u_0^{(j)}(x); \qquad u^{(j)}(c,t)=u^{(j+1)}(c,t), \quad \kappa_j u^{(j)}_x(c,t)=\kappa_{j+1} u^{(j+1)}_x(c,t)

variables.meaning: scalar field defined on each spatial subdomain whose evolution is sought
one-dimensional position, partitioned into subdomains separated by interfaces
per-domain diffusion coefficient setting the smoothing rate

notes: Core is the piecewise linear diffusion (parabolic) equation with matching (continuity of field and weighted flux) at known interface locations plus prescribed far-field limits; the transform machinery (Fourier integrals, error functions) is solution technique, not the model.
